title: Lemonade (LMND) Is Down 14.4% After Q1 2026 Results Show Faster Growth And Lower Losses
text: Lemonade stock down 14.4% after Q1 2026 results despite faster growth and lower losses. Q1 2026 results: Revenue $258 million (71% growth), in-force premium $1.33 billion (32% growth), net loss $36 million improved from $62 million. Adjusted EBITDA loss $17 million, significant improvement from $47 million. Stock weakness suggests investors may have expected even better results or concerned about execution on profitability timeline. Market may be reacting to: Full-year EBITDA loss guidance suggests continued losses through most 2026, with profitability deferred to 2027. Premium valuation multiples: stock trading at 8.7x price-to-sales vs 1.1x insurance industry. Analyst consensus remained cautious Hold rating. Morgan Stanley upgrade offset by other analyst skepticism on valuation. Company maintaining 32% in-force premium growth guidance, 60%+ revenue growth. Pet insurance now largest segment surpassing $500 million in-force premium. Path to profitability clear but dependent on continued execution and premium growth rates.
